content:
Hey guys -
Just wanted a sanity check on this. Many of our internal apps don’t have a first-party CLI - so we built our own and it’s been working great - we use it somewhat a lot in agentic workflows as somewhat of a local mcp. Like I said it works great for that.
But now our lead developer and our CISO want to use this cli in the backend brains of some of our web apps. Not agentically. Basically having a web ui populated on backend using Python create a child process to run cli commands (which map to map to the api calls. Rather than using a native SDK or using Python requests library. Which is what we’ve been doing for years.
This seems insane to me. I’ve told them I think it’s insane. But they are adamant it’s fine. To me, this adds complexity in error handling, retries, memory inefficiency probably more issues I can’t think of. Maybe these are all minor problems and easily solvable with Codex/Claude code. But it just doesn’t make sense to me. Even if writing code is way easier today - it doesn’t mean we should write shit code.
Their explanation was - these are the commands that were running with our local ai agent so it is just easier so it makes debugging easier. So why didn’t we use AWS/gcloud cli 3 years ago to build the app???
I genuinely respect them - the Lead dev is excellent and I’ve seen him do great things over the years.
There has to be a reason no one builds web apps like this?? It just seems insane. Or am I wrong here?

  submitted by   /u/ProfessorHuman
[link]   [comments]
